market_summary: Risk-on to mixed-risk-on regime remains intact for the 30-day horizon: SPY +8.56%, QQQ +10.84%, and IWM +13.33% over 1m, all above MA50/MA200; breadth is broadening with small caps leading while TLT remains below major trend.

Current book still fits leadership: KLAC, AVGO, TSM, NVDA, AMZN, and GOOGL all outperform SPY on 1m or composite momentum and remain above MA50/MA200; only XOM shows clear short-term technical slippage while COST remains a low-volatility ballast.

No-go for rebalance today: cadence discipline is binding, qualitative refresh did not verify severe thesis damage across the refreshed subset, and expected edge does not clearly clear turnover drag for an off-cycle 30-day paper action.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows the clearest near-term damage in the current book: 1m return -7.08%, below MA50, and lagging SPY/QQQ materially, though it remains above MA200 and still positive over 3m/6m so damage is not yet severe enough to force an off-cycle change.

Momentum remains the weakest among current holdings on a composite basis (mom 4.33%, 1m 3.44%), implying lower expected alpha in a growth-led tape; however drawdown is shallow (-5.55%) and it still provides defensive ballast, so thesis is weakened but not broken.

Short-term moderation only: latest week was slightly negative and 63d drawdown is relatively deep (-20.37%), but trend support remains intact and recent quarterly fundamentals still show very strong revenue and earnings power; no severe thesis break verified.

thesis: Catalyst: none sufficient for an off-cycle trade; the highest-momentum holdings remain intact and the qualitative refresh did not surface clear earnings/guidance/regulatory damage in the refreshed equity subset.

Factor edge: turnover discipline itself is the edge today because portfolio alignment with the prevailing risk-on tape remains good, and the main laggards are not yet showing enough confirmed thesis failure to justify a non-scheduled change.

Variant view: while XOM and COST are weaker than the growth leaders, the market may simply be pausing rather than signaling durable factor rotation; preserving exposure to the existing diversified winner set is preferable to forcing marginal changes.
risks: Macro: a sudden rates shock, growth scare, or commodity spike could rotate leadership away from current mega-cap growth and semicap concentration.
Idiosyncratic: concentrated exposure to AI/semicap leaders raises single-theme earnings and supply-chain risk even without an immediate rebalance.
Execution/liquidity: not trading avoids unnecessary slippage and chasing extended winners, but deferring action could leave XOM/COST as relative drags if weakness persists into the next checkpoint.
falsifiable_checks: By the next scheduled rebalance window, if XOM remains below MA50 and continues to underperform SPY over the full period from 2026-04-22, treat it as a higher-priority replacement candidate.

By the next weekly checkpoint, if COST and GOOGL both lag SPY while at least one non-held replacement candidate maintains superior pack momentum without new qualitative damage, reassess the no-trade stance.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: the book is still aligned with a broadening but tech-favorable tape, and no refreshed qualitative datapoint created enough score separation to justify off-cycle turnover. Bull: semicap/AI leadership persists and doing nothing preserves exposure to the strongest existing trends. Bear: XOM and COST continue to dilute forward alpha, but that edge is not yet large or well-verified enough to overcome trading-cost drag and weekly cadence discipline in a paper monthly-hold process.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights—KLAC/AVGO/TSM/NVDA remain top momentum leaders vs SPY/QQQ; AMZN strengthened after prior add; XOM and COST are current relative weak spots; qualitative fundamentals available show NVDA exceptional profitability/ROE and GOOGL strong Q4 revenue/EPS, while unavailable fields for XOM/COST are marked UNKNOWN. Risk budget—IT exposure 53%, largest position 15%, no crypto, diversified across 5 sectors. Scenario stress—equity index shock hurts portfolio given growth tilt; rates shock likely pressures long-duration tech but breadth support helps; commodity shock benefits XOM but hurts consumer margins; USD shock is mixed with TSM FX/geopolitical sensitivity. Execution plan—hold unchanged until scheduled rebalance unless severe thesis damage emerges. Monitoring/kill-switch—watch XOM relative weakness, COST continued lag, and any negative earnings/guidance/regulatory surprise in NVDA/GOOGL; if a holding breaks trend and underperforms SPY through next checkpoint, elevate for replacement review.
